_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Conservative Management of Head Injury - Depressed Fracture
clinical_indications: No evidence of dural penetration and no significant intracranial hematoma
No wound infection or gross contamination
Simple depressed fractures in neurologically intact persons treated expectantly

clinical_thresholds.note: for non-surgical management of depressed fracture

preauth.label: Clinical Notes detailing the injury with admission Glasgow coma scale score

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes – details of accident, signs & symptoms, and planned line of treatment?
Was X-ray/ CT skull report submitted?
Was CT/ X-ray skull report suggestive of Depressed fracture?
Was CT head report of patient submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Were the indoor case papers submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure/ Operation notes submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: CT head report of patient not submitted at pre-authorization.
Patient intubated with GCS 9 or more without clear indication documented.
Clinical notes detailing accident, signs, symptoms, or planned line of treatment missing/incomplete at pre-authorization.
CT/X-ray skull report not suggestive of depressed fracture for a ST001B (Depressed Fracture) claim.
Depression depth not documented or exceeds 1cm without justification for conservative management.

Evidence of dural penetration, significant intracranial hematoma, frontal sinus involvement, wound infection/contamination, or gross cosmetic deformity found, making conservative management inappropriate for depressed fracture.
Indoor case papers missing at claim submission.
Detailed Procedure/Operation notes missing at claim submission.
Detailed Discharge Summary missing or does not mention date of follow-up at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: The brain is surrounded by cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), enclosed in meningeal covering, and protected inside the skull.
The fascia and muscles of the scalp provide additional cushioning to the brain.
Test results have shown that 10 times more force is required to fracture cadaveric skull with overlaying scalp than the one without.
surgical_options: Elevation of depressed skull fractures for open (compound) fractures.
Elevation of depressed fractures with thickness > of calvaria.
Elevation for fractures not meeting criteria for non-surgical management (e.g., dural penetration, intracranial hematoma, frontal sinus involvement, infection, gross cosmetic deformity).
